sections.heading: The Comprehensive Communication Audit
sections.body: Rate yourself honestly on each dimension (1 = needs significant improvement, 10 = expert). Be brutally honest — self-assessment only works if you don't inflate your scores.

code.snippet:
SPEAKING (rate each 1-10):
[ ] Clarity — Can I explain complex ideas simply?
[ ] Confidence — Do I speak up in meetings without excessive hedging?
[ ] Conciseness — Do I get to the point quickly?
[ ] Vocal variety — Do I vary my tone, pace, and volume?
[ ] Storytelling — Can I make a point with a compelling example?

LISTENING (rate each 1-10):
[ ] Full attention — Do I put away devices and focus?
[ ] Paraphrasing — Do I confirm understanding?
[ ] Questions — Do I ask clarifying questions?
[ ] Patience — Can I let someone finish without interrupting?
[ ] Empathy — Do I listen for emotions, not just words?

WRITING (rate each 1-10):
[ ] Structure — Is my writing organized and scannable?
[ ] Clarity — Is my main point obvious in the first sentence?
[ ] Brevity — Do I cut unnecessary words?
[ ] Tone — Does my writing sound professional but warm?
[ ] Action — Is there a clear next step for the reader?

NONVERBAL (rate each 1-10):
[ ] Eye contact — Do I maintain comfortable eye contact?
[ ] Posture — Do I stand/sit with confidence?
[ ] Gestures — Do I use open, natural gestures?
[ ] Congruence — Does my body language match my words?
[ ] Presence — Am I fully present, not distracted?

FEEDBACK & EMPATHY (rate each 1-10):
[ ] Giving feedback — Can I deliver it clearly and kindly?
[ ] Receiving feedback — Can I listen without defending?
[ ] Perspective-taking — Do I consider others' viewpoints?
[ ] Difficult conversations — Can I navigate conflict productively?
[ ] Emotional regulation — Can I manage my emotions in tough situations?
code.explanation: Tally your scores in each category. The category with the lowest average is your priority area. The category with the highest average is your strength to leverage.

sections.heading: Interpreting Your Results
sections.body: Your scores reveal patterns. Here's how to read them:
bulletPoints: Score 8-10: Strength — Leverage this. Help others develop in this area.
Score 5-7: Competent — Room for growth, but functional. Fine-tune.
Score 3-4: Development Area — This is holding you back. Prioritize improvement.
Score 1-2: Critical Gap — This is your biggest blind spot. Address immediately.

code.snippet:
EXAMPLE PROFILE:
Speaking: 7.5 (Competent — fine-tune)
Listening: 4.2 (Development Area — prioritize)
Writing: 8.0 (Strength — leverage)
Nonverbal: 5.8 (Competent — fine-tune)
Feedback & Empathy: 3.6 (Critical Gap — address immediately)

ANALYSIS:
- Strength: Writing (leverage this in emails, reports, documentation)
- Priority: Listening & Feedback — these are the biggest drags on relationships
- Action Plan: Practice active listening for 2 weeks, then give 3 pieces of SBI feedback

HIDDEN PATTERN: Strong writer but weak listener — common pattern. People who process well in writing sometimes struggle with the pace of spoken conversation.
code.explanation: The most powerful insights come from comparing categories. High writing + low listening suggests you're better at async than real-time communication. High speaking + low feedback suggests you're good at talking but not at honest dialogue.

sections.heading: Building Your 30-Day Communication Growth Plan
sections.body: Don't try to improve everything. Pick ONE area and commit for 30 days:

code.snippet:
30-DAY ACTIVE LISTENING PLAN:

Week 1: Awareness
- Track how many times you interrupt in conversations
- Practice the HEAR model in 1 conversation per day
- Goal: Notice your listening patterns

Week 2: Practice
- Use paraphrasing in every meeting: 'So what you're saying is...'
- Ask one clarifying question per conversation
- Goal: Build the habit of confirming understanding

Week 3: Deepen
- Practice global listening — notice emotions, not just words
- Use reflective listening: 'It sounds like you're feeling...'
- Goal: Listen at Level 2-3, not just Level 1

Week 4: Integrate
- Ask a colleague for feedback on your listening
- Apply all three levels in your most important relationship
- Goal: Make active listening automatic

MEASUREMENT: Ask a trusted person at Day 30: 'Have you noticed any change in how I listen?' Their answer is your success metric.
code.explanation: Thirty days of focused practice on ONE skill creates lasting change. Trying to improve five skills simultaneously results in improving none.

summary: Complete the communication audit across five dimensions: speaking, listening, writing, nonverbal, and feedback/empathy. Identify your highest strength (leverage it) and lowest gap (fix it). Build a 30-day growth plan focused on ONE skill. Measure progress through behavioral tracking and feedback from others.
